FIRST REPORT AT 7:09 AM EDT.

THE BACKWALL OF A STRUCTURE COLLAPSED NEAR POCAHONTAS DUE TO FLASH FLOODING. NEAR FALLS MILLS, FALLS MILLS ROAD HAD WATER FLOWING OVER IT LIKELY DUE TO FLOODING FROM BLUESTONE RIVER, BUT THE DEPTH WAS SHALLOW. A RESIDENT NEAR THIS AREA REQUESTED ASSISTANCE DUE TO HIS PROPERTY BEING FLOODED.

The Smith River near Woolwine (SMWV2) crested at 11.04 feet above moderate flood stage of 11 feet. This is the highest crest since Sept 2018, and nearly a 10-year flood event. The river rose nearly 9 feet within 6 hours of the crest.
Several roads are flooded and impassible by creeks several feet out of their banks around the community of Ararat, including a portion of Raven Rock Road.
Spring Branch Road was destroyed and is impassible where it crosses the Ararat River. This was caused by rapid and intense flooding from heavy rainfall.
White Sulphur Springs Road is impassible due to flooding of the Ararat River rising rapidly several feet out of its banks.
Corrects previous flash flood report from 1 SW Flat Rock. Hamburg Street is flooded up to a foot deep over the road due to the Ararat River overflowing its banks.

Livestream video indicates the Ararat River flooding out of its banks several inches deep onto Riverside Drive and E. Oak Street. A gas station at the corner of E. Pine Street and Riverside Drive is surrounded by flood waters.
Hamburg Street is flooded up to a foot deep over the road due to the Ararat River overflowing its banks.

The Ararat River at Mt. Airy, NC (ARLN7) crested at 23.71 feet, rising above the moderate flood stage of 22 feet. The river rose nearly 10 feet in the six hours leading up to the crest due to heavy rain across Carroll and Patrick Counties.
Several inches of water flowing over Paul Goodwin Road just of U.S. 221 in the Baldwin area. Mud and rocks were also washed out into the road. The road had to be closed in both direction by NCDOT.
